items.title: Support H.3754 / S.2344 road safety cameras and prepare new home rule petition
items.summary: Council supports state House Bill H.3754 and Senate Bill S.2344 on traffic regulation using road safety cameras and requests the City Manager prepare a new home rule petition that would let Cambridge install and operate cameras to enforce red light, speeding, and other moving violations. Adopted 8-0-1-0 with Councillor Zusy voting Present.
items.body: THAT THE CAMBRIDGE CITY COUNCIL SUPPORTS HOUSE BILL H.3754, AND SENATE BILL S.2344, "AN ACT RELATIVE TO TRAFFIC REGULATION USING ROAD SAFETY CAMERAS; AND THAT THE CITY MANAGER BE AND HEREBY IS REQUESTED TO WORK WITH RELEVANT DEPARTMENTS TO REVIEW THE STATE BILLS REFERENCED AS WELL AS THE PREVIOUSLY FILED HOME RULE PETITION AND PREPARE A NEW HOME RULE PETITION THAT WOULD ALLOW CAMBRIDGE TO INSTALL AND OPERATE CAMERAS TO ENFORCE RED LIGHT, SPEEDING, OR OTHER MOVING VIOLATIONS AS OUTLINED IN H.3754 AND S.2344 AND THE PRIOR HOME RULE.

rationale.geo.scope:citywide: road safety cameras would be installed citywide
rationale.topic.area:public_safety: traffic enforcement and road safety cameras
rationale.transport.mode:drive: cameras enforce red light, speeding, moving violations affecting drivers
rationale.transport.subject:traffic_calming: automated enforcement of speeding/red lights is a traffic-calming tool
rationale.fiscal.kind:fine: cameras would issue fines for moving violations
rationale.civic.flag:home_rule_petition: PREPARE A NEW HOME RULE PETITION

items.title: Simplify processes for paying election workers by stipend
items.summary: A policy order asking the City Manager to work with the Election Commissioners to review IRS guidance and consult other municipalities to simplify how Cambridge pays poll/election workers, including paying them by stipend without mandatory withholdings or municipal retirement contributions. Adopted 9-0-0.
items.body: THAT THE CITY MANAGER BE AND HEREBY IS REQUESTED TO WORK WITH RELEVANT CITY DEPARTMENTS AS WELL AS THE ELECTION COMMISSIONERS TO REVIEW GUIDANCE FROM THE IRS AND CONSULT WITH OTHER MUNICIPALITIES IN ORDER TO SIMPLIFY OUR PROCESSES FOR PAYING ELECTION WORKERS AND PAY WORKERS BY STIPEND WITHOUT MANDATORY WITHHOLDINGS OR MUNICIPAL RETIREMENT CONTRIBUTIONS

rationale.geo.scope:citywide: election worker pay process is citywide
rationale.work.employer:government: election workers are paid by the City (municipal employer)

items.title: Amend Zoning Ordinance Section 4.50: religious, educational, and childcare uses as-of-right citywide
items.summary: Amends Cambridge Zoning Ordinance Section 4.50 to allow religious purposes; educational purposes on land owned/leased by the Commonwealth, its agencies/subdivisions, a religious sect or denomination, or a nonprofit educational corporation; and child care center, school-aged child care program, family child care home, or large family child care home as defined in MGL Ch. 15D, sec. 1A as-of-right in all zoning districts. Passed to a Second Reading.
items.body: AMEND SECTION 4.50 OF THE CAMBRIDGE ZONING ORDINANCE AS FOLLOWS WITH THE INTENT OF PERMITTING THE USE OF LAND FOR THE FOLLOWING PURPOSES AS-OF-RIGHT IN ALL ZONING DISTRICTS: RELIGIOUS PURPOSES; EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ON LAND OWNED OR LEASED BY THE COMMONWEALTH OR ANY OF ITS AGENCIES, SUBDIVISIONS OR BODIES POLITIC OR BY A RELIGIOUS SECT OR DENOMINATION, OR BY A NONPROFIT EDUCATIONAL CORPORATION; AND FOR A CHILD CARE CENTER, SCHOOL-AGED CHILD CARE PROGRAM, FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME, OR LARGE FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME AS DEFINED IN SECTION 1A OF CHAPTER 15D OF MASSACHUSETTS GENERAL LAWS.

history.action: Ordinance Committee public hearing — voted favorably to forward to full Council with favorable recommendation

rationale.doc.kind:ORD: Ordinance — AMEND SECTION 4.50 OF THE CAMBRIDGE ZONING ORDINANCE
rationale.doc.status:approved: RESULT: Passed to a Second Reading [VV9]
rationale.doc.stage:second_reading: PASS TO A SECOND READING IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026
rationale.geo.scope:citywide: AS-OF-RIGHT IN ALL ZONING DISTRICTS
rationale.housing.tenure:any: zoning change affects all property in all districts
rationale.household.kids:under_5: CHILD CARE CENTER, FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME

items.title: Raise residential parking permit fee to $75, $25 for low-income; remove senior exemption; cap at 2 cars/household
items.summary: A policy order proposing major changes to the residential parking permit program: a $75 base fee, a subsidized $25 fee for residents in affordable housing, on SNAP, or low-income, removal of the existing senior exemption, and a cap of 2 permits per household (down from 4). Tabled 8-0-1-0 with Councillor Al-Zubi voting Present.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to raise the fee of the parking permit program for all residents to $75, consider how to include a self-identified check off option so as not to increase administrative costs for a subsidized fee of $25 for residents who live in affordable housing, are enrolled in a program such as SNAP or are low income, remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program and lower the number of cars that individual residents are allowed to get a residential parking permit for from four to two.

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons

rationale.doc.stage:tabled: PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026
rationale.doc.sponsor:simmons: CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS — sponsor of original
rationale.geo.scope:citywide: residential parking permit program is citywide
rationale.housing.tenure:any: permit changes affect both renters and owners
rationale.housing.affordability:inclusionary: residents who live in affordable housing
rationale.fiscal.kind:parking_fee: raise the fee of the parking permit program
rationale.fiscal.kind:permit_fee: fee of the parking permit program
rationale.demo.age:senior_65plus: remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program
rationale.demo.income:low: subsidized fee of $25 for residents who ... are low income
rationale.demo.income:means_tested: enrolled in a program such as SNAP

items.title: Process for expanding free early child care offerings (means-tested models, non-City funding)

Adopted as amended by voice vote (Councillor Zusy voted Present).
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which Cambridge can expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming, and exploration of non-City funding sources, from the state or foundations.
